key_points: Transport helps move people and goods from one place to another
Communication helps exchange information across distances
Roadways are the most commonly used mode of transport in India
Railways connect major cities and transport goods across long distances
Ports and airports help India connect with other countries through trade
examples: Golden Quadrilateral connects Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai and Kolkata
Ganga river forms National Waterway No.1
Mumbai port is one of the largest ports in India
Air transport connects remote regions like the North-East
formulas: Balance of Trade = Value of Exports − Value of Imports
exam_tips: Remember the different types of transport: road, rail, water and air
Know important ports and waterways in India
Understand why transport is important for trade
Practice map-based questions related to ports and transport routes
